name: Puerto de La Sía por Picón Blanco – circular por los puertos pasiegos
description: This difficult 31.7-mile mountain bike route climbs 6004 feet through the Valles Pasiegos to Picón Blanco for panoramic views.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Soba offers a diverse landscape characterized by rugged terrain, prominent limestone massifs, and dynamic river systems within the Natural Park of Collados del Asón. The region features deep river valleys, lush forests, and alpine meadows, shaped by glacial action and extensive karstic processes. This topography provides a variety of challenging and scenic mountain bike routes. Best mountain bike trails in Soba The most popular mountain bike trail is Puerto de La Sía por Picón Blanco – circular por los puertos pasiegos, a 31.7 miles (51.1 km) trail that takes 5 hours 26 minutes to complete. This challenging route cycles through the Pasiegos valleys, offering panoramic views. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Nacimiento del río Asón y Alto de la Posadía – circular por el Parque Natural de los Collados del Asón, a difficult 14.3 miles (23.0 km) path. This route explores the natural park, featuring significant elevation changes and scenic vistas. Local mountain bikers also love the Yera Station – Aján River Waterfalls loop from Veguilla, a 53.4 miles (85.9 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 7 hours 22 minutes. Mountain biking around Soba is defined by rugged terrain, towering peaks, deep river valleys, and lush forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to technical descents. The routes in Soba are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 300 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Soba's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Soba?
A: There are over 60 mountain bike trails in Soba, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels. You'll find everything from moderate paths to highly technical descents.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Soba?
A: Yes, Soba offers a few easier mountain bike trails. While many routes are moderate to difficult, there are 4 trails specifically categorized as easy, providing a good starting point for less experienced riders.

Q: What are some of the most challenging mountain bike routes in Soba?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, Soba has 32 difficult mountain bike routes. A notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e999688920/puerto-de-la-sia-por-picon-blanco-circular-por-los-puertos-pasiegos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de La Sía por Picón Blanco – circular por los puertos pasiegos&lt;/a&gt;, a 51.1 km trail with significant elevation gain through the Pasiegos valleys. Another demanding route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31485766" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Yera Station – Aján River Waterfalls loop from Veguilla&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 85.9 km of varied terrain.

Q: Can I find circular mountain bike routes in Soba?
A: Yes, many mountain bike routes in Soba are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1000405687/nacimiento-del-rio-ason-y-alto-de-la-posadia-circular-por-el-parque-natural-de-los-collados-del-ason" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nacimiento del río Asón y Alto de la Posadía – circular por el Parque Natural de los Collados del Asón&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 23.0 km circular path exploring the natural park. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14992433" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Grazing area – Plaza de Las Machorras loop from Las Machorras&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 29.7 km loop.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails that pass by waterfalls in Soba?
A: Absolutely. The region is known for its impressive waterfalls. You can find routes that lead to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3921167" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Asón Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, also known as "Cola del Caballo," or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3963021" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aján River Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31485766" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Yera Station – Aján River Waterfalls loop from Veguilla&lt;/a&gt; specifically includes these falls.

Q: Which mountain bike routes offer the best scenic viewpoints in Soba?
A: Soba's rugged terrain provides numerous panoramic vistas. Routes that ascend to areas like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4347749" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de la Posadía&lt;/a&gt; or through the Pasiegos valleys, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e999688920/puerto-de-la-sia-por-picon-blanco-circular-por-los-puertos-pasiegos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de La Sía por Picón Blanco&lt;/a&gt;, offer breathtaking views of the surrounding landscapes, including the Circo de Hondojón and the Soba valley.

Q: What natural attractions can I explore while mountain biking in Soba?
A: Beyond waterfalls and viewpoints, Soba is rich in natural wonders. You can encounter the dramatic karst landscapes and glacial features within the Natural Park of Collados del Asón. The region is also famous for its caves, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839749" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cullalvera Cave&lt;/a&gt;, and offers challenging ascents to summits like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839693" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castro Valnera Summit&lt;/a&gt; for those seeking higher altitudes.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy the most about mountain biking in Soba?
A: The mountain biking experience in Soba is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars. Reviewers often praise the diverse and rugged terrain, the stunning natural beauty of the Collados del Asón Natural Park, and the variety of trails that cater to different skill levels, from lush forests to alpine meadows.

Q: Is Soba suitable for mountain biking with children or families?
A: While many routes in Soba are challenging, there are 4 easy trails that could be suitable for families or those riding with children, depending on their experience level. It's advisable to check the specific route details and elevation profiles to ensure they match your family's capabilities.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Soba?
A: The best time for mountain biking in Soba is generally during the spring, summer, and early autumn months when the weather is milder and trails are clear. The region's higher elevations can experience snow and colder conditions in winter, making some routes less accessible or more challenging.

Q: Are there any moderate mountain bike trails in Soba?
A: Yes, Soba has a significant number of moderate mountain bike trails, with 29 routes falling into this category. These trails offer a good balance of challenge and accessibility, making them suitable for intermediate riders. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24568892" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pastries from La Dolce Vita loop from Agüera&lt;/a&gt;, a 33.0 km route.
tips: El Picón Blanco is a well-known mountain pass, very popular among road cyclists, although it is gaining popularity among donkey lovers, since it can be reached by a forest track in good condition for those who prefer to avoid asphalt. From here it is usually connected to the port of La Sía, the most popular among the ports of Pas.

faqs.question: How difficult is the Puerto de La Sía por Picón Blanco mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This is a difficult mountain bike ride, requiring very good fitness and advanced riding skills. You should be prepared for technical sections, with some parts rated STS S3-S6, and very steep ramps where pushing your bike may be necessary. The ascent to Picón Blanco, especially from Espinosa de los Monteros, features an average gradient of 9.1% with ramps up to 18%.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: The route offers diverse terrain typical of the Pasiego mountains. You'll encounter dirt tracks, forest paths, and trails winding through natural surroundings. While some sections are well-maintained, be ready for very steep ramps and aggressive terrain, particularly on the climb to Picón Blanco.

faqs.question: What are the main scenic highlights along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll experience the picturesque Valles Pasiegos with lush meadows, dense beech and oak forests, and traditional villages. Key highlights include the stunning vistas from the Alto del Portillo de La Sía (1,234m) and the spectacular panoramic views from the summit of Picón Blanco (1,521m). On clear days, you can see the Cantabrian Sea and even the distant Picos de Europa. The summit also features abandoned military installations.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 51 km long with over 1,800 meters of elevation gain. It typically takes around 5 hours and 26 minutes of riding time to complete, but plan for longer to account for breaks and enjoying the views.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to ride the Puerto de La Sía por Picón Blanco trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to ride this trail is generally during the warmer, drier months to ensure clearer paths and the best panoramic views from Picón Blanco. Avoid periods of heavy rain or snow, as conditions can become very challenging and visibility may be poor.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Puerto de La Sía por Picón Blanco mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: Specific parking information for the start of this circular route is not detailed, but you can typically find parking in nearby towns like Espinosa de los Monteros, which serves as a common access point for the region's trails. Always check local regulations before parking.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally allowed on many trails in the Pasiego mountains, it's crucial to keep them under control, preferably on a leash, especially when passing through traditional grazing areas or near wildlife. Given the route's difficulty and technical sections, consider if it's suitable for your dog's fitness and paw health.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees for this trail?
faqs.answer: No specific permits or entrance fees are generally required to ride this mountain bike trail. However, always respect private property and local regulations, especially when passing through rural areas or near traditional cabins.

faqs.question: What essential gear should I bring for mountain biking this route?
faqs.answer: Given the route's difficulty and length, you should bring a well-maintained mountain bike suitable for technical terrain, a helmet, appropriate protective gear, plenty of water and snacks, a repair kit, a first-aid kit, and navigation tools (like a GPS device or the komoot app). Layers of clothing are also recommended due to changing mountain weather.

faqs.question: Is the Puerto de La Sía por Picón Blanco route suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: No, this route is not suitable for beginners or families. It is rated as a 'difficult' mountain bike ride requiring 'very good fitness' and 'advanced riding skills' due to its steep gradients, technical sections, and significant elevation gain. There are sections where pushing your bike may be necessary.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other known cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. You'll find sections that overlap with the Ruta de Carlos V, [CIMA CA08] Asón - La Sía, Ruta 43 Merindad de Montija, [CIMA BU09] Picón Blanco, [CIMA BU10] La Sía, and the Camino de Bustihierro, among others.

faqs.question: Are there any specific tips for tackling the challenging climbs on this route?
faqs.answer: The ascents, particularly to Picón Blanco, are notoriously challenging. Be prepared for sustained climbs with gradients up to 18%. Pacing yourself is key, and don't hesitate to dismount and push your bike on the steepest or most technical ramps. Ensure your bike is in excellent condition with appropriate gearing for steep ascents.
